title: Stop the Tipping Cycle From MCP_Dual During Non-Recoverable Operator Station Motor Fault Recovery
summary: Use the operator station HMI to open the MCP_Dual screen and press CYCLE STOP to stop the tipping cycle as part of the documented recovery process for a non-recoverable operator station motor fault that cannot be recovered in software and requires manual reset.
when_to_use: Use this procedure during recovery of a non-recoverable operator station motor fault when the documented manual process instructs the user to stop the tipping cycle from the operator station HMI.
not_for: Do not use this as a complete recovery procedure for the non-recoverable fault; the provided source excerpt includes only the initial HMI actions.
Do not use this for faults that can be recovered via software.
Do not continue beyond stopping the tipping cycle if the remaining manual reset steps are not available from the source.

responsibilities: Navigate to the MCP_Dual screen using F3.
Press CYCLE STOP to stop the tipping cycle.
Escalate or defer if additional manual reset steps are required but not available in the provided source.

safety_notes: The source states that some faults in this category cannot be recovered via software and require manual reset.
This runbook covers only the initial stopping actions from the provided source excerpt.
Because the documented recovery is incomplete in the supplied excerpt, stop and escalate before attempting unsupported follow-on recovery actions.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: A non-recoverable operator station motor fault recovery is in progress.
The operator station HMI is available.
The documented recovery requires stopping the tipping cycle from MCP_Dual.

visual_references.description: Operator station HMI screen snippet showing axis controls and RESET instruction in the non-recoverable fault recovery context; use as the primary visual reference for MCP_Dual-related recovery actions.

steps.title: Navigate to the MCP_Dual screen
steps.purpose: Open the operator station HMI screen identified by the source for the non-recoverable fault recovery sequence.
steps.instruction: At the operator station HMI, navigate to the "MCP_Dual" screen using F3.
steps.expected_result: The MCP_Dual screen is displayed on the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station HMI shows the MCP_Dual screen referenced by the recovery procedure.
steps.failure_condition: The MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed, F3 does not open the expected screen, or the HMI is unavailable.

actions.label: Open MCP_Dual
actions.exact_action: On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "MCP_Dual" screen (F3).

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station HMI screen snippet associated with non-recoverable fault recovery; use it to identify the MCP_Dual/operator station control context and visible axis controls.
Operator station HMI view showing manual mode axis controls and RESET control in the same recovery context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator station HMI is not accessible.
F3 does not navigate to the MCP_Dual screen.
The displayed screen does not match the recovery context supported by the source.
Additional recovery steps are needed but are not available in the provided source excerpt.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 1. On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "MCP_Dual" screen (F3).
Artifact appears on page 84 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_5_7_1_non_recoverable_fault_operator_station_motors.

steps.title: Press CYCLE STOP
steps.purpose: Stop the tipping cycle as the next documented action in the non-recoverable operator station motor fault recovery process.
steps.instruction: On the MCP_Dual screen, press CYCLE STOP to stop the tipping cycle.
steps.expected_result: The tipping cycle is stopped from the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipping cycle stops after CYCLE STOP is pressed.
steps.failure_condition: The tipping cycle does not stop, the CYCLE STOP control is unavailable, or further recovery cannot proceed from the provided source information.

actions.label: Stop tipping cycle
actions.exact_action: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the tipping cycle.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station HMI recovery-context screen snippet to orient to the control area associated with stopping the tipping cycle.
Operator station HMI view in the same non-recoverable fault recovery context; use as supporting visual context for the operator station controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: Pressing CYCLE STOP does not stop the tipping cycle.
The CYCLE STOP control cannot be located on the operator station HMI.
The fault requires additional manual reset actions that are not present in the provided source excerpt.
The condition appears non-recoverable in software and the next documented manual steps are unavailable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 2. Press CYCLE STOP to stop the tipping cycle.
Artifact appears on page 84 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_5_7_1_non_recoverable_fault_operator_station_motors.

success_criteria: The operator station HMI is navigated to the MCP_Dual screen using F3.
CYCLE STOP is pressed on MCP_Dual.
The tipping cycle is stopped as part of the documented non-recoverable fault recovery process.
healthy_conditions: The MCP_Dual screen is accessible from the operator station HMI.
The CYCLE STOP control can be used from the MCP_Dual screen.
The tipping cycle stops after the documented HMI action is performed.
failure_conditions: The fault cannot be recovered in software and requires manual reset.
The MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed from the operator station HMI.
The tipping cycle does not stop after pressing CYCLE STOP.
The provided source excerpt does not include the remaining manual reset steps needed to continue recovery.
escalation_guidance: Escalate or defer further recovery if additional manual reset steps are required but not available in the provided source.
Escalate if the MCP_Dual screen cannot be reached or the operator station HMI is unavailable.
Escalate if pressing CYCLE STOP does not stop the tipping cycle.
Do not invent or perform unsupported follow-on recovery actions beyond the source-backed steps.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: If a fault occurs that is not able to be recovered via software, such as a motor overload fault, the station needs to be manually reset.
1. On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "MCP_Dual" screen (F3).
2. Press CYCLE STOP to stop the tipping cycle.
